extra_model_fields.priority_hint: Implement chg_1 through chg_4 before chg_5 and chg_6 to minimize rework.
verification_checklist: calculation_trace.json has numeric intermediate values for every row
validation_report.txt documents d_min, d_max, formula, and fallback rules
sensitivity flags present for values within 10% of thresholds

extra_model_fields.priority_hint: Implement chg_1 through chg_4 before chg_5 and chg_6 to minimize rework.
verification_checklist: calculation_trace.json has numeric intermediate values for every row
validation_report.txt documents d_min, d_max, formula, and fallback rules
sensitivity flags present for values within 10% of thresholds

planned_changes.description: Create calculation_trace.json containing a machine-readable per-row object for each cell with explicit numeric fields: flood_depth_m, slope_pct, road_distance_m, flood_excess_m, slope_excess_pct, hard_rule_exclusion (boolean), road_distance_rank, intermediate_scaled_distance (numeric), accessibility_score (numeric or null if excluded). Include metadata: timestamp, software version, and provenance (which input files/steps produced each numeric value).

Write validation_report.txt (or validation_report.json) summarizing automated checks: CSV parseability, row_count, eligible_rows, excluded_rows, mapping of column names, any missing inputs, decision_basis (hard-rule logic), normalization parameters (d_min, d_max), fallback rules if no eligible rows, and results of sensitivity tests (identify rows within 10% of thresholds).

Update shelter_accessibility_table.csv to include standardized contract column names and an explicit accessibility_score. Proposed normalization: compute d_min and d_max as min and max of distance_to_road_m among non-excluded rows; if no non-excluded rows, compute over all rows and note the fallback in validation_report. Formula to document and apply: scaled_distance = (distance_to_road_m - d_min) / (d_max - d_min); accessibility_score = clip(1 - scaled_distance, 0, 1). Provide per-row numeric intermediate fields (d_min, d_max, scaled_distance) in the CSV or calculation_trace.json.

Add an uncertainty_notes column flagging rows whose flood_depth_m or slope_pct are within 10% of the exclusion thresholds and include a brief sensitivity statement (e.g., 'flood_depth within 10% of threshold; measurement error of +/-0.1m could change eligibility').

Add a short README or header section (and a link from CSV) describing where to find calculation_trace.json and validation_report.txt and summarizing how to reproduce scores; include exact commands or pseudocode to recompute accessibility_score from calculation_trace.json.

Run an internal verification step that re-parses the updated CSV and calculation_trace.json to verify that per-row accessibility_score values recompute identically from the documented formula and normalization parameters; include the verification results in validation_report.txt.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Add missing reproducibility artifacts and explicit normalized accessibility_score per rubric: provide separate calculation_trace and validation_report artifacts, standardize CSV column names, and document normalization parameters and sensitivity handling.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Add missing reproducibility artifacts and explicit normalized accessibility_score per rubric: provide separate calculation_trace and validation_report artifacts, standardize CSV column names, and document normalization parameters and sensitivity handling.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submitted CSV correctly applied the binary exclusion rules (flood_depth > 1m OR slope > 10%) and documented per-row checks and calculation traces. However, the deliverable is missing several rubric-required items that reduce independent reproducibility: no separate calculation_trace.json or standalone validation_report.txt artifacts, column names do not match the contract exactly, and there is no explicit normalized accessibility_score with documented normalization parameters (d_min, d_max, formula).
